proposals.lens: MIRROR-PRIOR-ART: name and shape every verb to track microsandbox's `msb` CLI (confirmed from the local clone's clap enum in crates/cli/bin/main.rs), with shapes cross-checked against smolvm `machine` and krunvm so anyone fluent in those tools is instantly fluent in ours, and so the documented escape hatch (adopt/fork microsandbox later) is a near-rename rather than a re-learn. We diverge from `msb` ONLY where a LOCKED project decision forces it (ephemeral-by-default instead of msb's named-persistent default; podman-labels instead of msb's SQLite; --network none default instead of msb's permissive ingress; SSH-agent-forward instead of msb's in-guest sshd; no snapshot/volume/registry/MCP breadth). The tool keeps the noun-less flat verb form of `msb <verb>` (NOT smolvm's `machine <verb>` noun-prefix) because msb is the named escape hatch and flat verbs are what we graduate INTO. doctor (verb zero) is the one place msb has no analog, so its name comes from agent-sandbox's `ags doctor`.

verbs.behavior: Verb zero. Capability-probe the host before any launch: /dev/kvm exists and is accessible (KVM group membership), `crun --version` reports +LIBKRUN, the crun-krun handler/symlink is present, libkrun.so.1 and an ABI-compatible libkrunfw load, and `podman info` plus a `podman --runtime crun-krun run ... true` smoke test pass. Prints each check with PASS/FAIL and the validated podman/crun/libkrun versions. Exits 0 only if every gate passes.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only: probes, launches nothing persistent (smoke test uses --rm). Hard-fails with an actionable fix message (e.g. `usermod -aG kvm`) rather than letting a later run fail cryptically. Distinct precondition exit code, never conflated with a workload exit code.
verbs.provenance: Name and role taken verbatim from agent-sandbox `ags doctor` (msb has no doctor analog); checks shaped by microsandbox/libkrun capability probes (krun_check_nested_virt / krun_has_feature / krun_get_max_vcpus) and sbx's documented `usermod -aG kvm` first-run failure.

verbs.signature: sandbox run [--name N] [--keep] [--cpus N] [--memory MiB] [--network none|localhost] [--publish HOST:GUEST]... [--mount HOST:GUEST[:ro]]... [--rlimit nofile=N,nproc=N] [--ssh-agent] [--tty] [--timeout DUR] [--json] <image> -- <cmd...>
verbs.behavior: The primary one-shot verb: create a fresh disposable microVM from <image>, create a per-sandbox git worktree on the host and bind it read-write at the workdir, run <cmd...> in the foreground (blocking like krun_start_enter), and propagate the workload's exit code verbatim. UNNAMED runs are ephemeral and auto-removed on exit; this is the default path. The flag set after `--` is the workload command (msb's exact `-- <cmd...>` shape).
verbs.safe_default: --rm-equivalent is the default (locked ephemeral-by-default); --network none; all mounts read-only EXCEPT the tool-created worktree (the only :rw); --security-opt no-new-privileges + conservative --cap-drop; conservative krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations stamped by the single centralized birth function; --publish is a silent no-op guarded against under --network none. Layer-1 (--rm clean exit) + Layer-2 (trap INT/TERM/EXIT calling `podman rm -f -v`) cleanup both attach here.
verbs.provenance: `msb run <image> -- <cmd>` (confirmed in crates/cli/lib/commands/run.rs) — ephemeral-when-unnamed is msb's own behavior; reinforced by smolvm `machine run` (ephemeral default) and krun's one-shot run = create+start. `--keep` mirrors msb's named-vs-unnamed persistence split as a flag rather than a separate verb.

verbs.signature: sandbox exec [--workdir DIR] [--env K=V]... [--user U] [--tty] [--timeout DUR] [--ssh-agent] <name> -- <cmd...>
verbs.behavior: Run an additional command in an already-running named sandbox (auto-starts it if stopped, exactly as msb's resolve_and_start does). With no `-- <cmd>` in tty mode, attach to a default shell. This is the 'enter the workload' path for the opt-in persistent case.
verbs.safe_default: Only operates on sandboxes carrying our managed-by label; inherits the parent sandbox's --network none and read-only posture (adds no new mounts). Secrets stay out of the guest: --ssh-agent forwards the host SSH_AUTH_SOCK (bind socket + set env var inside) so keys never enter the VM.
verbs.provenance: `msb exec <name> -- <cmd>` (crates/cli/lib/commands/exec.rs, auto-start semantics confirmed), name shared with smolvm `machine exec`, krunvm `start <name> <cmd>`, and crun-vm's SSH-based exec --as <user>.

verbs.behavior: List sandboxes managed by this tool by querying podman directly (`podman ps --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us>`), reconciling live state for free with no DB. Columns: id, status, ports/published-localhost, worktree, created, persist-flag. `--all` includes exited/persistent ones.
verbs.safe_default: Selection is EXCLUSIVELY by our mandatory label set, never by name substring (the claude-code-sandbox anti-pattern). Read-only. `--json` / stable columns derived straight from `podman ps --format`.
verbs.provenance: msb `list` (visible_alias `ls`, confirmed in main.rs). We keep the long name `list` as primary like msb's enum, with `ls` alias (also smolvm `ls`, sbx `ls`).

verbs.behavior: Show status for one sandbox (or all if name omitted): running/exited, resource caps, published localhost ports, worktree path, labels. The introspection counterpart to `list`, pulling from `podman inspect --format`.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only, label-scoped. `--json` (single object or array) is the machine-readable contract callers compose around; exit code distinguishes not-found from found.
verbs.provenance: msb `status` (command name `status`, visible_alias `ps`, confirmed in main.rs mapping ps::PsArgs). Aliased `ps` like msb; role overlaps smolvm `machine status` and arrakis `list`.

verbs.behavior: Stream or tail captured stdout/stderr from a (running or exited) sandbox via `podman logs`, for post-mortem on a crashed workload.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only; label-scoped so it refuses to dump logs of containers we don't manage. Tail defaults to a bounded line count.
verbs.provenance: msb `logs <name>` (crates/cli/lib/commands/logs.rs); identical name/shape to claude-code-sandbox `logs -f -n` and gh-runner's log model.

verbs.behavior: Gracefully stop running named (persistent) sandboxes — SIGTERM then SIGKILL after timeout (`podman stop`). State/worktree kept; does not remove. --force kills immediately.
verbs.safe_default: Label-scoped; graceful by default (grace before kill), matching msb's SIGTERM-then-SIGKILL-after-timeout. Never silently discards a worktree with unpushed commits.
verbs.provenance: msb `stop <name...> -f -t` (crates/cli/lib/commands/stop.rs, force+timeout confirmed); same name in smolvm, sbx, arrakis, podman.

verbs.behavior: Remove persistent sandboxes from inventory (`podman rm -f -v`) and tear down their tool-created worktree. --force stops-then-removes a running one. This is the explicit teardown for anything created with --keep.
verbs.safe_default: Label-scoped. Worktree deletion is guarded by a strict path-safety check (inside our managed root, not a symlink, not repo root) and refuses/warns on unpushed commits unless forced; --keep-worktree preserves it. Mirrors smolvm's verification-gated, ordered teardown.
verbs.provenance: msb `remove` (visible_alias `rm`, confirmed in main.rs); aliased `rm` like msb / smolvm / sbx. Worktree-loss guard answers sbx's documented `rm` footgun.

verbs.behavior: Label-driven cleanup backstop (Layer 3) for the crash case the --rm and trap layers miss: `podman ps -a -q --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us> --filter status=exited` then `podman rm -f -v`, with an optional age cut via the sandbox.created label / `--until`. Idempotent; safe to run at the top of every invocation, as msb fires its reaper on every command.
verbs.safe_default: Touches ONLY containers carrying our managed-by label (never host-global state — the arrakis anti-pattern). Disarms the EXIT trap before sweeping to avoid double-cleanup (gh-runner lesson). --dry-run prints what it would remove. Skips sandboxes explicitly labelled persistent unless they are dead/orphaned.
verbs.provenance: Severity-ladder name from claude-code-sandbox's nuclear `purge`/`clean`, but modelled on microsandbox's reap_stale_sandboxes()/spawn_reaper() and gh-runner-krunvm's prefix-reaper — re-cast as label-driven per our locked design.

proposals.rationale:
Through the mirror-prior-art lens, microsandbox's `msb` is the canonical surface to track because it is BOTH the closest peer (libkrun microVMs for agent code) AND our documented graduation/escape hatch — so picking names a future `msb` adopter already knows makes the migration a near-rename. I confirmed msb's exact enum from the local clone (crates/cli/bin/main.rs): Run, Create, Start, Stop, List(ls), Status(ps), Metrics, Remove(rm), Exec, Copy(cp), Logs, Pull, Ssh, Inspect, Volume, Snapshot, Install. I adopt the seven that fit a single-user accident-threat-model sandbox verbatim — run, exec, list/ls, status/ps, logs, stop, remove/rm — preserving msb's `<image> -- <cmd...>` run shape and its visible aliases, since those aliases are also the smolvm/sbx vocabulary, maximizing cross-tool familiarity.

Three deliberate, LOCKED-decision divergences from msb, each minimized to stay close to the prior art: (1) Ephemeral-by-default. msb's `run` is ephemeral only when UNNAMED and persistent when NAMED; I keep exactly that mechanism but make it explicit via `--keep`/`--name`, so persistence is the labelled opt-in our spec mandates rather than a separate verb — closer to msb than smolvm's `-d/--detach` would be. (2) No snapshot/volume/registry/install/metrics/copy/create/start verbs. These are msb's platform-breadth and two-phase surface; they are out of scope (snapshot = our worktree/git's job; volumes/registry/install = state we refuse; create/start split is unneeded once run is the ephemeral one-shot). Dropping them keeps the script single-file-auditable and is itself the boundary our graduation signal names. (3) doctor and reap have no msb-CLI analog (msb hides its reaper and has no doctor), so I borrowed those two names from the adjacent prior art that DOES surface them — `ags doctor` and the claude-code-sandbox `purge`/`clean` severity ladder — while implementing reap on msb's own reap_stale_sandboxes pattern.

Every safe-by-default posture (--network none, read-only-except-worktree, label-only selection, ssh-agent forward, conservative krun.cpus/ram_mib, ordered path-safe teardown) is where we are STRICTER than msb's permissive engine defaults, per the accident-not-adversary threat model — but the names and shapes a user types stay msb-native, satisfying the lens. The relevant source confirming the surface is /var/home/tom/mecattaf/dotfiles/skills/microvm/references/inspiration/microsandbox/crates/cli/bin/main.rs and crates/cli/lib/commands/{run,exec,stop,remove,logs}.rs.

proposals.lens: SAFETY-MINIMALIST: the smallest verb set in which the safe path is the only expressible path. Ephemeral is not a mode you select, it is the only thing `run` does; persistence is a separate, louder verb that must be typed out. Cleanup is not trusted to one mechanism — `reap` is a first-class backstop verb, not a hidden internal. `doctor` is verb zero and gates everything. Every verb selects sandboxes exclusively by the mandatory managed-by label set (never by name substring), and every verb that lists is machine-readable by default. Fewer verbs, each with exactly one unambiguous meaning, so a clumsy agent (or a clumsy human) cannot accidentally express the dangerous thing.

verbs.behavior: Verb zero. Runs the precondition/capability probe and refuses to certify the host if any gate fails: /dev/kvm exists and is accessible (KVM group membership), crun was built with +LIBKRUN (parse `crun --version` feature string), the crun-krun handler/symlink is present, libkrun.so.1 and libkrunfw.so.<abi> are loadable and ABI-compatible, and a `--network none` smoke run of `true` under --runtime=krun succeeds. Emits the validated podman/crun-krun/libkrun versions so guest surprises are diagnosable. Every other verb invokes the same checks (cheaply) before doing work; `doctor` is the standalone, verbose, --json-able form. Pure read-only, mutates nothing.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only; never launches a real workload, never touches host-global state. Fails CLOSED with a distinct exit code and an actionable message ('run usermod -aG kvm') rather than letting a later run fail cryptically.
verbs.provenance: name verbatim from agent-sandbox (`ags doctor`) and confirmed by textcortex `config`-as-introspection; check content from crun (feature_string LIBKRUN, /dev/kvm), libkrun (krun_has_feature/krun_get_max_vcpus capability probes), libkrunfw (ABI/soname check), sbx (`usermod -aG kvm` first-run failure).

verbs.signature: sandbox run [--cpus N] [--memory MiB] [--rw PATH] [--ro PATH:ro] [--publish HOST:GUEST] [--network none|localhost] [--ssh-agent] [--timeout DUR] [--json] -- <cmd...>
verbs.behavior: THE primary and only birth verb. Creates a fresh per-sandbox git worktree on the host (the tool makes it; never accepts a pre-made one), funnels everything through the ONE centralized spawn function that stamps the mandatory label set (sandbox.managed-by, .created, .id, .worktree) plus safe-by-default isolation flags, then `podman run --rm --runtime=krun` in the FOREGROUND. The container is unnamed and ALWAYS --rm: ephemeral is not a flag, it is the only behavior this verb has. On clean exit --rm removes it (layer 1); an INT/TERM/EXIT trap calls `podman rm -f -v <id>` for the interactive-crash case (layer 2), disarming itself first (`trap '' EXIT`) to avoid double-cleanup. Propagates the workload's exit code verbatim, reserving distinct codes for setup (125) vs exec (126) vs not-found (127) vs doctor/precondition failure. Resource caps go through krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations (validated integers, clamped to >128 MiB / <=16 vCPU) not cgroup flags.
verbs.safe_default: --network none, mounts read-only except the single tool-created worktree (bound rw with :Z), --security-opt no-new-privileges, conservative --cap-drop, small caps (1 cpu / 512 MiB) unless raised, destructive git guarded inside the guest, ssh-agent forwarded only on explicit --ssh-agent so keys never enter the guest. There is NO way to make `run` persist — that requires a different verb.
verbs.provenance: `run` = ephemeral one-shot from microsandbox (unnamed `msb run` auto-removed), smolvm `machine run`, ERA `vm temp`, gh-runner-krunvm microvm_run; one-shot run=create+start collapse from podman/crun; worktree-creation from agent-sandbox git.rs + packnplay; --network none default from sbx Locked Down / Codex `read-only`; trap-disarm-first from gh-runner-krunvm.

verbs.signature: sandbox persist [--cpus N] [--memory MiB] [--rw PATH] [--ro PATH:ro] [--publish HOST:GUEST] [--network none|localhost] [--ssh-agent] --name NAME -- <cmd...>
verbs.behavior: The explicit, separate, louder opt-in to a surviving sandbox — deliberately a DIFFERENT verb from `run` so the ephemeral default can never be accidentally turned off by a stray flag. Uses the SAME centralized spawn function and SAME mandatory labels, but additionally stamps a sandbox.persist=true label and a sandbox.name label, OMITS --rm, and detaches (-d) so it survives the CLI process. Requires an explicit --name (idempotent recreate via --replace). Prints the parseable localhost endpoint(s) so ssh/Caddy/Tailscale compose around it. This is the only path that produces something `reap` must later clean.
verbs.safe_default: Inherits every safe-by-default isolation flag from the spawn function (network none, ro mounts, caps). Prints a LOUD warning that this sandbox survives and is the user's responsibility to `down`/`reap`. The persist label is what scopes the reaper, so a persistent sandbox is always discoverable and never invisible. Still cannot mount anything rw except the tool-created worktree.
verbs.provenance: explicit-persistence-as-opt-in is the dominant prior-art pattern (awesome-agent-sandboxes, microsandbox NAMED vs unnamed split, crun-vm `--persistent` with loud warning); detached background birth from microsandbox `create` / smolvm `--detach` / sbx `create`; loud-warning-on-persist from crun-vm.

verbs.behavior: List every managed sandbox by querying podman EXCLUSIVELY on the mandatory label (`podman ps --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us>`), never by name substring. Stable columns by default (id, status, persist, ports, created, worktree) and --json for machine consumption, derived straight from `podman ps --format`. --all includes exited/orphaned records. Read-only; this is also how a human or agent discovers what `reap` would act on.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only, label-scoped: it can only ever see sandboxes the tool itself stamped, so it can never report or implicate an unrelated container. Machine-readable is the default, not an opt-in.
verbs.provenance: `ls` name from sbx and microsandbox (`msb ls`); stable-columns/--json contract from microsandbox `status --format json` and sbx `ls` column set; label-only selection is the explicit fix for claude-code-sandbox's name-substring weakness.

verbs.behavior: Graceful, intentional teardown of a PERSISTENT sandbox the user created with `persist`. Selects by sandbox.name label (or --all over the persist label), sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL after a timeout (`podman stop` then `podman rm -f -v`). Before removing, guards the worktree: refuses to discard a worktree with unpushed/uncommitted commits unless forced, so a clumsy `down` cannot silently lose work. This is the everyday, expected cleanup; `reap` is the backstop for when this never ran.
verbs.safe_default: Graceful by default (SIGTERM grace window before kill). Will NOT silently destroy a worktree with unpushed commits. Label-scoped so it can only stop things the tool manages. Distinct from the nuclear `reap`.
verbs.provenance: `down`/`stop` as the gentle, intentional teardown from arrakis `stop`, smolvm `machine stop`, microsandbox `stop`; the unpushed-commits guard from sbx (`rm` drops unfetched commits footgun) and claude-code-sandbox shadow-repo motivation; graceful-then-kill timeout from arrakis reapProcess / microsandbox.

verbs.behavior: The first-class label-driven BACKSTOP — the whole reason it is a named verb rather than a hidden internal is that it is the safety net for the cases the other two layers miss (SIGKILLed `run`, crashed CLI, a `persist` sandbox the user forgot, a VMM segfault that bypassed --rm). Runs a cheap reconcile at the TOP of every other invocation too (so the field stays clean without a daemon), but is also invokable directly. Lists by the mandatory label, hard-kills/removes any managed sandbox whose container is dead/exited/orphaned (`podman rm -f -v`), and with --older-than uses the sandbox.created label as an age GC. Path-safety-gated: any worktree rm is refused unless the path is non-empty, strictly inside the managed root, not the root, and not a symlink. --dry-run prints what it would reap.
verbs.safe_default: Operates ONLY on containers carrying our managed-by label — never host-global state (the explicit anti-pattern from arrakis wiping all tap*/br0). Idempotent. rm -rf is gated by a strict is-safe-path check (mirrors smolvm). --dry-run available so the nuclear action can be previewed. Because podman labels are the only source of truth, this needs NO state file/DB to reconcile.
verbs.provenance: `reap`/backstop concept from gh-runner-krunvm prefix reaper (relabelled to label-driven), microsandbox spawn_reaper-on-every-invocation, smolvm cleanup_orphaned_ephemeral_vms with PID+path verification; severity-ladder (down < reap) from claude-code-sandbox stop<clean<purge; age GC from podman `container prune --filter until=` and microsandbox --max-duration; path-safety guard from smolvm is_safe_cache_path.

verbs.behavior: Run an additional command inside an already-running PERSISTENT sandbox (selected by sandbox.name label), for debugging/inspection. For an ephemeral `run`, there is intentionally nothing to exec into — the whole point is one foreground workload that dies. Passes arbitrary agent-written code via base64 over `-- bash -c` to dodge quoting bugs. Does not weaken any isolation flag the sandbox was born with.
verbs.safe_default: Only attaches to label-managed, already-running persistent sandboxes; cannot create or resurrect one (no auto-start that would silently extend a sandbox's life). Inherits the sandbox's existing isolation; adds no new mounts or network. Keys still never enter the guest (ssh-agent forward only).
verbs.provenance: `exec` from podman/microsandbox/smolvm/sbx; base64 command-transport trick from ERA; 'enter the workload vs the host' discipline from crun-vm; no-auto-start choice diverges deliberately from microsandbox's resolve_and_start to keep lifetimes explicit.

proposals.rationale: Seven verbs, ordered by how a safety-first user meets them: doctor (gate) -> run (the safe default and the common case) -> persist/down/exec (the explicit, louder persistent path) -> ls/reap (observe and backstop). The minimalism is deliberate and load-bearing: ephemeral-vs-persistent is split across TWO VERBS (`run` vs `persist`) rather than a `--rm`/`--persist` flag pair, because a flag is exactly the thing a clumsy agent fat-fingers — making persistence a different word you must consciously type is the cheapest way to make the safe path the only easily-expressible one. `run` has no off switch for ephemerality at all. I rejected the common `create`/`start` split (krun, krunvm, smolvm): a two-phase lifecycle invites a half-born sandbox and a second thing to forget to clean, which is pure downside for a single-user accident model — podman's one-shot `run` collapses it. I rejected separate `stop`/`clean`/`purge` ladders (claude-code-sandbox) and `destroy`/`destroy-all` (arrakis), folding intentional teardown into one `down` and making the nuclear path the single first-class `reap`; the severity ladder collapses to exactly two rungs (intentional `down`, backstop `reap`) because more rungs is more surface for the wrong one. I deliberately KEPT `reap` as a user-facing verb even though it also auto-runs on every invocation — the safety-minimalist bet is that the backstop must be auditable and invokable, not hidden. `ls` is label-only and machine-readable by default, closing claude-code-sandbox's name-substring hole and satisfying the --json contract. Snapshot/restore/pause/resume/cp/logs/upload/download/config/policy/login are all dropped: snapshot/restore (arrakis) is the biggest complexity sink and unneeded for accident-containment (git/worktree does backtracking); network policy DSLs (microsandbox, sbx) are anti-exfiltration features outside our threat model; login (sbx) and config files (claude-code-sandbox) violate no-login/no-state-file. Every verb selects only by the mandatory managed-by label, fails closed, and touches only resources the tool stamped — so the surface itself encodes the blast-radius-containment threat model.

proposals.lens: PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL: every verb is the one a podman/docker user would reach for first. The tool is a thin disciplined wrapper over `podman run --runtime=krun`, so its surface deliberately echoes podman's own verb set (run/exec/ps/rm/logs/stop/inspect), with only the additions podman lacks for this niche (doctor as verb-zero, reap as the label-driven backstop, and an explicit ephemeral/persist split expressed as flags on run rather than new nouns). Where a podman verb has a more colloquial docker alias, the docker name is offered as an alias so muscle memory from either tool lands. Anything podman already does well is passed through, not reinvented.

verbs.behavior: Verb-zero precondition gate. Checks /dev/kvm exists and is accessible (KVM group membership), `crun --version` reports +LIBKRUN, the crun-krun handler/symlink is present, libkrun.so.1 and libkrunfw are loadable and ABI-compatible, and runs a one-shot `podman --runtime=krun run --rm <base> true` smoke test. Prints validated podman/crun/libkrun versions. Exits non-zero with an actionable message per failed check; emits machine-readable status under --json.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only: inspects host capability and never mutates anything or launches a real workload (only a `true` smoke container). Refuses nothing, breaks nothing; pure diagnosis.
verbs.provenance: agent-sandbox `doctor` (verb name lifted verbatim) + crun-vm/sbx KVM-access preflight; capability-probe pattern adapted from libkrun krun_check_nested_virt/krun_has_feature.

verbs.signature: sandbox run [--name N] [--keep] [--cpus N] [--memory MiB] [--publish HOST:GUEST]... [--network none|loopback] [--mount HOST:GUEST[:ro]]... [--workdir DIR] [--env K=V]... [--ssh-agent] [-it] [--json] <image> [-- <cmd...>]
verbs.behavior: The primary one-shot verb: create + start a microVM in the foreground via `podman --runtime=krun run --rm`, exec the workload, block until it exits, propagate the guest exit code verbatim. Goes through the single centralized birth function that stamps the mandatory label set (sandbox.managed-by/.created/.id/.worktree) and safe-by-default isolation flags, creates the per-sandbox git worktree itself and bind-mounts it rw (:Z) while everything else is ro, validates/clamps --cpus and --memory into krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations. Installs trap on INT/TERM/EXIT for crash cleanup. `-- <cmd>` is passed through (base64-transported to dodge quoting bugs).
verbs.safe_default: Ephemeral by default (--rm), --network none, read-only mounts except the tool-created worktree, no-new-privileges, conservative cap-drop, conservative cpu/ram caps, secrets never enter the guest. Network/publish/persistence are all explicit opt-ins, never the default.
verbs.provenance: podman `run` (create+start in one shot, the exact verb wrapped) + microsandbox/smolvm `run` ephemeral-by-default; --keep flag echoes microsandbox's named-vs-unnamed persistence split rather than ERA's separate `temp` verb.

verbs.behavior: Run an additional command inside an already-running labeled sandbox, streaming stdout/stderr and returning the command's exit code. Thin wrapper over `podman exec`; resolves the target by label/id, refuses targets not bearing our managed-by label.
verbs.safe_default: Operates only on sandboxes we own (label-gated); inherits the running sandbox's existing isolation, opens no new network or mounts, defaults to the workload user not root.
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `exec` (verb name and shape verbatim); microsandbox/smolvm/sbx all expose the same `exec`.

verbs.behavior: Stream or dump captured stdout/stderr of a labeled sandbox via `podman logs`, demultiplexing as podman does. --follow tails live; --tail bounds history.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only observation; label-gated to our own sandboxes; no mutation, no side effects.
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `logs` (verbatim); microsandbox `logs`, claude-code-sandbox `logs` (-f/--tail).

verbs.behavior: List managed sandboxes by querying `podman ps --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us>` with stable columns (id, status, ports, worktree, created, persist-flag). Default shows running; --all includes exited. --json / stable-columns mode derived straight from `podman ps --format`. `ls` is an accepted alias.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only; selection is EXCLUSIVELY by our mandatory label set, never by name substring; never lists or touches non-managed containers.
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `ps` (verbatim, with `ls` alias from microsandbox/sbx/smolvm `list`/`ls`); label-filter discovery from podman + gh-runner-krunvm microvm_list (label-backed, not name-prefix).

verbs.behavior: Dump the full configuration and runtime status of one sandbox (labels, annotations, OCIRuntime, mounts, published ports, worktree path) via `podman inspect --format`. Default human key/values; --json emits podman's raw inspect JSON. The metadata source-of-truth introspection verb.
verbs.safe_default: Read-only; label-gated to managed sandboxes; pure introspection with no side effects.
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `inspect` (verbatim); also krunvm `inspect` and microsandbox/arrakis `inspect`.

verbs.behavior: Gracefully stop a running sandbox via `podman stop` (SIGTERM then SIGKILL after timeout). For ephemeral sandboxes this triggers --rm teardown; for --keep (persistent) sandboxes the container record and its labels survive for a later start. --all stops every managed running sandbox.
verbs.safe_default: Graceful by default (SIGTERM first, bounded grace before SIGKILL); label-gated so only our sandboxes are signaled; preserves the host worktree (never deletes a worktree on stop).
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `stop` (verbatim, --time); smolvm/microsandbox/sbx/arrakis all expose `stop`.

verbs.behavior: Re-start a previously persisted (--keep) stopped sandbox via `podman start`, boots the microVM from its stored definition and reattaches. Exists only because persistence is opt-in; ephemeral sandboxes never reach a startable stopped state.
verbs.safe_default: Operates only on labeled persistent sandboxes; reuses the original isolation flags/annotations stamped at birth, opening no new surface; refuses to start anything lacking our managed-by label.
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `start` (verbatim); the create/start split from crun OCI model and krunvm/smolvm/microsandbox `start`.

verbs.behavior: Remove one or more sandboxes from podman via `podman rm`. -f stops-then-removes a running one. The targeted teardown for persistent (--keep) sandboxes the user is done with. After removing the container, removes the associated tool-created worktree ONLY after a path-safety check (inside our managed root, not a symlink, not empty/root) and ONLY if it carries no unpushed git commits (else it is left in place with a loud warning). `delete` is an accepted alias.
verbs.safe_default: Operates only on labeled sandboxes; never force-removes without -f; never silently destroys a worktree with unpushed commits; path-guarded rm so the reaper/rm can never escape the managed root.
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `rm` (verbatim, -f/-a; `delete` alias from crun/krunvm/arrakis `delete`); unpushed-commit guard from sbx's clone-rm footgun + smolvm is_safe_cache_path.

verbs.behavior: The label-driven cleanup backstop for the crash case --rm and traps miss. Queries `podman ps -a --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us>` and hard-cleans managed sandboxes whose container is exited/dead/orphaned (`podman rm -f -v`), then applies the same path-guarded worktree removal as `rm`. --until adds an age backstop against the sandbox.created label. --dry-run reports what would be reaped without acting. Designed to also run as a cheap reconcile sweep at the top of every invocation (out-of-process / non-blocking) so users get their exit code immediately.
verbs.safe_default: Acts ONLY on our mandatory-label set and only on dead/orphaned/aged sandboxes (never a healthy running one); idempotent; disarms the EXIT trap first to avoid double-cleanup; touches only resources we labeled, never host-global state; honors the same no-unpushed-commit worktree guard.
verbs.provenance: microsandbox spawn_reaper/reap_stale_sandboxes + smolvm orphan-sweep + gh-runner-krunvm prefix reaper, all re-keyed onto podman LABELS instead of a DB/name-prefix; severity-ladder name from claude-code-sandbox `clean`/`purge` (we collapse to one `reap`).

verbs.behavior: Copy files between host and a running sandbox via `podman cp`, with progress sent to stderr so redirects stay clean. A convenience for retrieving workload artifacts without opening a mount.
verbs.safe_default: Label-gated to managed sandboxes; absolute paths required (cwd is unreliable under the engine); does not widen the sandbox's mount surface; progress on stderr keeps stdout machine-parseable.
verbs.provenance: podman/docker `cp` (verbatim); microsandbox/smolvm/sbx `cp` with the `name:path` syntax and stderr-progress ergonomic from smolvm.

proposals.rationale:
Through the podman-mental-model lens the design goal is zero surprise: a podman/docker user should guess our verbs correctly on the first try. So the spine is podman's own surface, used as near-passthroughs: run, exec, logs, ps, inspect, stop, start, rm, cp. We wrap `podman run --runtime=krun` directly, so `run` is the obvious primary verb and `--runtime` semantics (create+start in one shot) map cleanly onto our ephemeral --rm default. The ephemeral/persist split is expressed the podman-native way — as a flag on run (--keep, stamping a persistence label) plus the natural start/stop/rm lifecycle that flag unlocks — rather than inventing a new noun like ERA's `temp` or krunvm's split create. This keeps persistence an explicit opt-in without growing the verb count.

Only three things are NOT pure podman, because podman genuinely lacks them for this niche: (1) `doctor` as verb-zero, taken from agent-sandbox, because podman has no krun/KVM precondition gate and late cryptic failures are the cautionary tale across the prior art; (2) `reap` as the label-driven backstop, because podman is daemonless and a SIGKILLed `podman run` orphans an exited-but-registered container — the exact gap microsandbox/smolvm/gh-runner-krunvm built reapers for, which we get cheaply by keying on our mandatory labels instead of a DB; and (3) the worktree/unpushed-commit guards bolted onto rm/reap, because the tool creates the worktree and must not let clumsy agent code (or our own cleanup) destroy unpushed work.

Aliases (ls for ps, delete for rm) are offered so muscle memory from either docker or podman lands. I deliberately did NOT surface krunvm's `changevm`/`config`, arrakis's snapshot/pause/resume, microsandbox's volume/snapshot/install, or any network-allowlist DSL — those are out of our accident-not-adversary threat model and would break the single-file-auditable graduation discipline. Every safe-default is fail-closed (--network none, ro mounts, conservative caps, label-gated selection) precisely where the engine is fail-open, and selection is exclusively by our mandatory label set — never by name substring, which is the documented weakness in claude-code-sandbox and sbx.

revisions.lens: MIRROR-PRIOR-ART: name and shape every verb to track microsandbox's `msb` CLI (confirmed enum: Run, Stop, List/ls, Status/ps, Remove/rm, Exec, Copy/cp, Logs, Ssh) so anyone fluent in msb is instantly fluent in ours, and the documented escape hatch (adopt/fork microsandbox later) is a near-rename rather than a re-learn. Diverge from msb ONLY where a LOCKED project decision forces it; borrow doctor from agent-sandbox and reap (the one msb hides) from microsandbox's own reap_stale_sandboxes pattern.
revisions.critique_of_others:
SAFETY-MINIMALIST (`run`/`persist` as TWO verbs): the core bet — that ephemeral-vs-persistent split across two verbs is safer than a flag because "a flag is what a clumsy agent fat-fingers" — is the weakest claim in any of the three proposals, and it is risky for THIS project specifically. (1) It contradicts the locked decision verbatim: the spec says "persistence is an explicit opt-in verb/FLAG that stamps a label" — flag is named as an acceptable form, and the LOCKED list later says "an explicit opt-in verb/flag." More importantly, a separate verb `persist` does NOT reduce fat-finger risk; it just relocates it. The agent's failure mode is not "typed --keep by accident" (you don't accidentally type a flag that requires a value like --name); it is "ran the wrong command." `persist` is a whole command an agent can wrongly choose just as easily. The real safety property — that the DEFAULT is ephemeral — is identical whether persistence is `--keep` or `persist`; both require an explicit affirmative act. So `persist` buys no safety over `run --keep` while it (a) breaks the msb near-rename escape hatch (msb expresses this exactly as named-vs-unnamed on one `run`), and (b) forces a `down`+`exec`+`start`-less lifecycle that is now asymmetric and confusing (you `persist` but you `down`, not symmetric with podman). (2) `exec` with NO auto-start is a genuine usability regression dressed as safety: microsandbox's resolve_and_start auto-start exists because the alternative is the user getting "not running, go run start first" — and SAFETY-MINIMALIST has no `start` verb at all, so a stopped persistent sandbox is stranded: you cannot exec into it and cannot restart it; your only move is `down` (destroy) and re-`persist` (new identity, new worktree). That is a worktree-orphaning trap, the exact footgun the project's worktree-per-sandbox rule is trying to prevent. (3) Folding teardown into one `down` plus base64-over-bash-c command transport baked into the verb DESCRIPTION is over-specification of an implementation detail at the surface-design layer.

PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL: strongest of the two others and closest to mine, but it over-grows toward podman-completeness in a way that fights the single-file-auditability graduation signal. (1) It surfaces NINE+ verbs including `start`, `inspect`, AND `cp` — `start`/`inspect` are defensible, but `cp` is a real liability for THIS threat model: `podman cp` into a running krun microVM is a mount-surface-adjacent escape hatch, and the proposal even admits it needs absolute-path and stderr-progress caveats. For accident-containment where the worktree IS the artifact channel (git push out, not file copy), `cp` is unneeded surface that contradicts "read-only mounts where possible." (2) Both `ps` AND `inspect` as separate verbs duplicates introspection; msb collapses this into status/ps (list = many, status/ps = one+detail). Two introspection verbs is more surface for no threat-model gain. (3) Offering `delete` and `ls` aliases is harmless but the proposal's instinct to "pass through anything podman does well" is precisely the un-disciplined growth the graduation signal warns against — passthrough is not free when each verb must carry label-gating + worktree guards + safe-default enforcement. Every passthrough verb is code you must audit. (4) Expressing persist as `--keep` on run is RIGHT and I agree with it over SAFETY-MINIMALIST's two-verb split.

revised_verbs.behavior: Verb zero. Capability-probe the host before any launch: /dev/kvm exists and is accessible (KVM group membership), `crun --version` reports +LIBKRUN, the crun-krun handler/symlink is present, libkrun.so.1 and an ABI-compatible libkrunfw load, and `podman info` plus a `podman --runtime crun-krun run --rm <base> true` smoke test pass. Prints each check PASS/FAIL with validated podman/crun/libkrun versions. Exits 0 only if every gate passes. Every other verb runs the same checks cheaply before doing work; doctor is the standalone verbose --json-able form.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Read-only: probes, launches nothing persistent (smoke test uses --rm). Hard-fails CLOSED with an actionable fix message (e.g. `usermod -aG kvm`) and a DISTINCT precondition exit code never conflated with a workload exit code.
revised_verbs.provenance: Name and role verbatim from agent-sandbox `ags doctor` (msb has no doctor analog). Check content from crun (+LIBKRUN feature string, /dev/kvm), libkrun capability probes (krun_check_nested_virt / krun_has_feature / krun_get_max_vcpus), libkrunfw ABI/soname check, sbx's documented `usermod -aG kvm` first-run failure. Unanimous across all three lenses.

revised_verbs.signature: sandbox run [--name N] [--keep] [--cpus N] [--memory MiB] [--network none|loopback] [--publish HOST:GUEST]... [--mount HOST:GUEST[:ro]]... [--env K=V]... [--workdir DIR] [--ssh-agent] [-it] [--timeout DUR] [--json] <image> [-- <cmd...>]
revised_verbs.behavior: The primary one-shot verb: create a fresh disposable microVM from <image> via `podman run --rm --runtime=krun`, create a per-sandbox git worktree on the host and bind it rw (:Z) at the workdir, run <cmd...> in the foreground (blocking), propagate the workload exit code verbatim. Goes through the single centralized birth function stamping the mandatory label set (sandbox.managed-by/.created/.id/.worktree) and safe-by-default isolation flags. UNNAMED runs are ephemeral and auto-removed (the default path); `--keep` (requires `--name`) stamps sandbox.persist=true + sandbox.name and OMITS --rm so the sandbox survives — persistence is the labelled opt-in, expressed msb-style as named-vs-unnamed on ONE run verb rather than a second verb.
revised_verbs.safe_default: --rm is the default (locked ephemeral-by-default); --network none; all mounts read-only EXCEPT the tool-created worktree (the only :rw); --security-opt no-new-privileges + conservative --cap-drop; conservative krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations (clamped >128 MiB, <=16 vCPU); --publish a guarded no-op under --network none. Layer-1 (--rm clean exit) + Layer-2 (trap INT/TERM/EXIT -> `podman rm -f -v`, disarming itself first). --keep prints a LOUD warning that the sandbox survives and is the user's responsibility to `rm`/`reap`.
revised_verbs.provenance: `msb run <image> -- <cmd>` (crates/cli/lib/commands/run.rs) — ephemeral-when-unnamed is msb's own behavior; `--keep` mirrors msb's named-vs-unnamed persistence split as a flag, NOT a separate verb. CONVERGED with PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL (also `--keep` on run); rejected SAFETY-MINIMALIST's separate `persist` verb (relocates rather than reduces fat-finger risk, breaks msb near-rename, strands stopped sandboxes).

revised_verbs.behavior: Re-start a previously kept (`run --keep`) stopped sandbox via `podman start`, booting the microVM from its stored definition and reattaching. Exists ONLY because persistence is opt-in; ephemeral runs never reach a startable stopped state.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Operates only on label-managed persistent sandboxes; reuses the original isolation flags/annotations stamped at birth, opening no new mounts or network; refuses anything lacking our managed-by label.
revised_verbs.provenance: podman/docker `start` verbatim; krunvm/smolvm/microsandbox all expose start. ADOPTED from PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL: I originally omitted start, but SAFETY-MINIMALIST's design proves the gap — without start, a stopped persistent sandbox can only be destroyed-and-recreated, orphaning its worktree. The create/start split is unneeded for the EPHEMERAL path (run collapses it) but necessary for the PERSISTENT path. msb itself has Start in its full enum; I was wrong to drop it given --keep exists.

revised_verbs.signature: sandbox exec [--workdir DIR] [--env K=V]... [--user U] [-it] [--ssh-agent] [--timeout DUR] <name> -- <cmd...>
revised_verbs.behavior: Run an additional command in a kept (persistent) named sandbox; auto-starts it if stopped, exactly as msb's resolve_and_start does. With no `-- <cmd>` in tty mode, attaches a default shell. The 'enter the workload' path for the opt-in persistent case.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Operates ONLY on sandboxes carrying our managed-by label; inherits the parent's --network none and read-only posture, adds no new mounts; defaults to the workload user not root. Secrets stay out of the guest: --ssh-agent forwards host SSH_AUTH_SOCK so keys never enter the VM.
revised_verbs.provenance: `msb exec <name> -- <cmd>` (crates/cli/lib/commands/exec.rs, auto-start confirmed); name shared with podman/smolvm/sbx. KEPT msb's auto-start (resolve_and_start) and rejected SAFETY-MINIMALIST's no-auto-start choice: with a `start` verb present, auto-start is a convenience not a lifetime-extension footgun, and refusing it strands users.

revised_verbs.behavior: Stream or tail captured stdout/stderr from a running or exited sandbox via `podman logs`, for post-mortem on a crashed workload.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Read-only; label-scoped so it refuses to dump logs of containers we don't manage. Tail defaults to a bounded line count.
revised_verbs.provenance: `msb logs` (crates/cli/lib/commands/logs.rs); identical name/shape to podman, claude-code-sandbox `logs -f -n`. Unanimous across all three lenses.

revised_verbs.behavior: List sandboxes managed by this tool by querying podman directly (`podman ps --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us>`), reconciling live state with no DB. Stable columns by default (id, status, persist-flag, published-localhost ports, worktree, created); --all includes exited/persistent. This is also how a human or agent discovers what `reap` would act on.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Selection EXCLUSIVELY by our mandatory label set, never by name substring (the claude-code-sandbox anti-pattern). Read-only. Machine-readable (stable columns / --json from `podman ps --format`) is the DEFAULT, not an opt-in.
revised_verbs.provenance: msb `List` with visible_alias `ls` (confirmed main.rs:59). I now make `ls` the PRIMARY name (converging with SAFETY-MINIMALIST's `ls` and PODMAN's `ps` w/ ls-alias instinct) rather than `list`, because ls is the shorter muscle-memory token shared across msb/sbx/smolvm/podman-users. Dropped my original separate `status` verb — see disagreements.

revised_verbs.behavior: Dump full configuration and runtime status of ONE sandbox (labels, annotations, OCIRuntime, mounts, published localhost ports, worktree path) via `podman inspect --format`. The single-object introspection counterpart to the many-row `ls`.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Read-only; label-gated to managed sandboxes; pure introspection, no side effects. --json emits the machine-readable single-object contract; exit code distinguishes not-found from found.
revised_verbs.provenance: podman/docker `inspect` verbatim; krunvm/microsandbox/arrakis `inspect`. RENAMED from my original `status` to `inspect`, converging with PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL. msb's per-one verb is `status`/`ps`, but `inspect` is the more universally-correct-guess name (podman users reach for it first) and avoids the `ps`-means-process-list confusion. I keep exactly ONE detail verb (rejecting PODMAN's having BOTH ps AND inspect).

revised_verbs.behavior: Gracefully stop running kept (persistent) sandboxes — SIGTERM then SIGKILL after timeout (`podman stop`). State/worktree kept; does not remove. For ephemeral runs this is moot (they die in the foreground). --force kills immediately.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Label-scoped; graceful by default (grace before kill). Preserves the worktree (never deletes on stop). Never silently discards a worktree with unpushed commits.
revised_verbs.provenance: `msb stop -f -t` (crates/cli/lib/commands/stop.rs); same name in podman/smolvm/sbx/arrakis. Unanimous across all three lenses (SAFETY-MINIMALIST calls it `down` but the role is identical — see disagreements; I hold msb-native `stop`).

revised_verbs.behavior: Remove kept (persistent) sandboxes from inventory (`podman rm -f -v`) and tear down their tool-created worktree. --force stops-then-removes a running one. The explicit teardown for anything created with `--keep`.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Label-scoped. Worktree deletion guarded by a strict path-safety check (inside our managed root, not a symlink, not repo root, non-empty) and refuses/warns on unpushed commits unless forced; --keep-worktree preserves it.
revised_verbs.provenance: msb `Remove` with visible_alias `rm` (confirmed main.rs:70-71). I make `rm` PRIMARY (converging with PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL). Worktree-loss guard answers sbx's documented `rm` footgun + smolvm is_safe_cache_path. Unanimous in role across all three.

revised_verbs.behavior: Label-driven cleanup BACKSTOP (Layer 3) for the crash case --rm and traps miss (SIGKILLed run, VMM segfault, a forgotten --keep sandbox): `podman ps -a -q --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us> --filter status=exited` then `podman rm -f -v`, with an optional age cut via the sandbox.created label / `--until`. Runs as a cheap reconcile at the top of every invocation (so the field stays clean without a daemon) AND is invokable directly so the backstop is auditable, not hidden.
revised_verbs.safe_default: Touches ONLY containers carrying our managed-by label — never host-global state (the arrakis tap*/br0 anti-pattern). Idempotent. Disarms the EXIT trap before sweeping (gh-runner lesson). --dry-run previews. Skips healthy running sandboxes and persistent ones unless dead/orphaned. Same path-guarded worktree removal as `rm`.
revised_verbs.provenance: Severity-ladder name from claude-code-sandbox `purge`/`clean` (collapsed to ONE reap), modelled on microsandbox reap_stale_sandboxes()/spawn_reaper() and gh-runner-krunvm prefix-reaper, re-keyed to LABELS. Unanimous as a FIRST-CLASS verb across all three lenses — the strongest consensus point.

points_of_agreement: doctor is verb zero, read-only, fail-closed with a distinct precondition exit code and an actionable `usermod -aG kvm`-style message; every other verb runs the same checks cheaply first. Unanimous, no daylight.

reap is a FIRST-CLASS user-facing verb (not a hidden internal), label-driven, idempotent, --dry-run-able, age-GC via the sandbox.created label, runs as a cheap reconcile at the top of every invocation AND is directly invokable. All three lenses converged here independently — the single strongest consensus.

Every verb selects EXCLUSIVELY by the mandatory sandbox.managed-by label set, never by name substring; this closes the claude-code-sandbox/sbx name-substring hole and is what encodes the blast-radius threat model into the surface itself.
Listing verbs are machine-readable by DEFAULT (stable columns / --json straight from `podman ps --format`), not opt-in.

Safe-by-default isolation is uniform: --network none, read-only mounts except the single tool-created worktree, no-new-privileges, conservative cap-drop, conservative krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib (clamped), ssh-agent forward so keys never enter the guest.
The tool CREATES the worktree (never accepts a pre-made one) and any worktree removal is path-safety-gated AND unpushed-commit-guarded in both rm and reap.

Snapshot/restore/pause/resume/volume/registry/install/login/config/network-policy-DSL are ALL out of scope (out of threat model and/or violate no-state-file/no-login), and dropping them IS the single-file-auditability graduation discipline.
logs, stop, rm are near-passthrough podman/msb verbs with role agreement across all three; cleanup is three layers (--rm, trap, reap).
remaining_disagreements: persist as a separate VERB (SAFETY-MINIMALIST) vs --keep FLAG on run (me + PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL). I hold my ground, now with 2-of-3 consensus: a separate verb relocates fat-finger risk rather than reducing it (the failure mode is 'chose wrong command', not 'fat-fingered a flag that requires --name'), breaks the msb near-rename escape hatch (msb IS named-vs-unnamed on one run), and the locked spec itself says 'opt-in verb/FLAG'. The real safety property (ephemeral DEFAULT) is identical either way.

Whether `start` belongs at all (I now include it, originally didn't; SAFETY-MINIMALIST omits it entirely). I moved TO including it: without start, a stopped --keep sandbox can only be destroyed+recreated, orphaning its worktree — a footgun worse than the verb's cost. SAFETY-MINIMALIST's omission is its design's most concrete bug.

exec auto-start (me + PODMAN, via msb resolve_and_start) vs no-auto-start (SAFETY-MINIMALIST). I hold auto-start: with a `start` verb present, auto-start is convenience, not a hidden lifetime-extension; refusing it strands users for a threat (silent life-extension) that the loud --keep label and reap backstop already cover.

Verb NAMING: stop vs `down` (SAFETY-MINIMALIST). Minor; I hold msb/podman-native `stop` for the near-rename and because `stop` (gentle) vs `reap` (nuclear) is already a clear two-rung ladder without inventing `down`.

`cp` (PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL only). I REJECT it: `podman cp` into a running microVM is a mount-surface-adjacent channel that fights 'read-only mounts where possible', and the worktree (git push) is the sanctioned artifact path. This is the one place PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL over-grows past the graduation signal.
Two introspection verbs (PODMAN's ps AND inspect) vs one. I keep exactly ONE detail verb (`inspect`) plus `ls`; PODMAN's duplication buys no threat-model gain.

revisions.lens: SAFETY-MINIMALIST: the smallest verb set in which the safe path is the only expressible path.
revisions.critique_of_others:
MIRROR-PRIOR-ART (msb tracking): Its source claims check out — I confirmed the msb clap enum (Run/Stop/List+ls/Status+ps/Remove+rm/Exec/Copy+cp/Logs...), that run.rs:224 removes unnamed/ephemeral sandboxes, and that BOTH run.rs and exec.rs call resolve_and_start. That last fact is the lens's quiet danger: it faithfully proposes `exec` auto-starts a stopped sandbox "exactly as msb's resolve_and_start does." For an accident-not-adversary model that is a genuine footgun — exec silently resurrecting a dead sandbox extends a lifetime the user thought was over and creates a second thing reap must catch. Tracking prior art is the right instinct, but here fidelity imports a behavior our threat model should reject; the lens should diverge as it does for --network none. Second concern: `--keep`/`--name` as flags on `run` reintroduces exactly the fat-finger surface I split into a separate verb. The lens defends this as "closer to msb," but msb's own mechanism (ephemeral-when-UNNAMED) means a stray `--name` flips persistence — that is the accident I am trying to make unexpressible. Otherwise this proposal is strong and its `reap`/`doctor` borrowings match mine.

PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL: Strongest on discoverability and most over-built on surface. Splitting lifecycle into run/stop/start/rm/cp/inspect (9+ verbs) is the podman-native answer, but it directly fights the project's single-file-auditable graduation discipline and multiplies the number of verbs a clumsy agent can misfire. Two specific risks for THIS project: (1) a first-class `start` verb only exists to resume a `--keep` stopped sandbox — it is pure persistence-machinery surface that the locked ephemeral-by-default decision should make rare, and every stopped-resumable sandbox is more orphan-state for reap; podman HAS start, but we shouldn't echo a verb whose only purpose is reanimating the non-default path. (2) `cp` widens the data-egress/ingress surface with a convenience that the worktree mount already covers — for accident-containment it is unnecessary verb count, and "copy into a running sandbox" is a mutation path I'd rather not offer. (3) Expressing ephemeral/persist as a `--keep` flag on run is the same fat-finger hole as MIRROR. Where PODMAN is RIGHT and I was too austere: dropping `inspect` entirely was wrong of me — podman/krunvm/arrakis all surface it, and a label/annotation/worktree-path introspection verb is genuinely needed for debugging and is purely read-only (zero added risk).
points_of_agreement: doctor is verb-zero in all three proposals, named verbatim from agent-sandbox ags doctor (msb has no analog), read-only, fails closed with a distinct exit code and an actionable usermod -aG kvm message; checks /dev/kvm access, crun +LIBKRUN, crun-krun handler, libkrun/libkrunfw ABI, and a --rm --network none `true` smoke run.

reap is a first-class, user-invokable, label-driven backstop (Layer 3) for the SIGKILL/crash case --rm and traps miss; it acts ONLY on the mandatory managed-by label set (never host-global state), is idempotent, disarms the EXIT trap first, supports --dry-run and an age cut (--until/--older-than via the sandbox.created label), and also runs cheaply at the top of every invocation. Modelled on microsandbox reap_stale_sandboxes/spawn_reaper, smolvm orphan-sweep, gh-runner-krunvm prefix-reaper, re-keyed onto podman labels.

run is the primary verb, ephemeral by default via --rm, foreground, propagates the guest exit code verbatim; goes through ONE centralized birth function stamping the mandatory labels (sandbox.managed-by/.created/.id/.worktree) and safe-by-default isolation (--network none, read-only mounts except the single tool-created worktree bound rw with :Z, no-new-privileges, conservative cap-drop, conservative krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations clamped to sane bounds).

Selection is EXCLUSIVELY by the mandatory managed-by label set, never by name substring (the claude-code-sandbox anti-pattern); every listing verb is machine-readable (--json / stable columns derived from podman ps --format) by default.

The tool CREATES the per-sandbox git worktree (never accepts a pre-made one); worktree removal is gated by a strict path-safety check (inside managed root, not root, not a symlink, non-empty) AND refuses to discard unpushed/uncommitted commits unless forced.

Drop snapshot/restore/pause/resume/volume/registry/install/login/config/network-policy-DSL — out of the accident-not-adversary threat model and a violation of no-state-file / single-file-auditability.
ls/list is label-only and machine-readable by default; logs is a read-only, label-gated podman logs passthrough with -f/--tail (I accept adding logs — see disagreements).

ssh-agent forwarding (only on explicit --ssh-agent) so keys never enter the guest; --publish/network is an explicit opt-in, never the default; ephemeral teardown is three layers: --rm + INT/TERM/EXIT trap (self-disarming) + reap.
remaining_disagreements: Ephemeral vs persist: I HOLD that these must be two different VERBS (run vs persist), not a --keep/--name flag on run. Both other lenses make it a flag because msb/podman do. But msb's own mechanism proves the hazard: persistence flips on a stray --name. A separate, louder verb you must consciously type is the cheapest way to make the unsafe path unexpressible-by-accident — which is the whole point of this project. This is my core lens contribution and I do not concede it.

exec auto-start: I HOLD that exec must NOT auto-start/resurrect a stopped sandbox, diverging deliberately from msb's resolve_and_start (which I confirmed run.rs and exec.rs both call). For accident-containment, silently extending a sandbox's life is exactly the surprise to avoid; exec should attach only to an already-running managed sandbox and fail closed otherwise. MIRROR imports msb's behavior here; I reject it.

Verb count / start & cp: I HOLD against a first-class `start` verb (its only job is reanimating the non-default persistent path; folds into persist --replace or is simply absent) and against `cp` (the worktree mount is the artifact path; cp is extra mutation surface for an accident model). PODMAN's discoverability argument is real but loses to single-file-auditability here.

I CONCEDE and MOVE TOWARD consensus on adding a read-only introspection verb. I had dropped it; PODMAN is right that inspect is needed and risk-free. I would add `inspect <name|id> [--json]` as a label-gated, read-only podman inspect passthrough — keeping my set at: doctor, run, persist, exec, ls, inspect, logs, down, reap (8 verbs). This is up from my original 7 but still far below PODMAN's ~10 and holds the safe-path-only invariant.

down vs stop/rm naming: minor. I keep `down` as the single intentional teardown of a persistent sandbox (collapsing podman's stop+rm), but I accept `stop`/`rm` aliases would aid muscle memory; the substance (one intentional teardown verb, graceful, unpushed-commit-guarded, distinct from nuclear reap) matters more than the name.

revisions.lens: PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL: every verb is the one a podman/docker user reaches for first; the tool is a thin disciplined wrapper over `podman run --runtime=krun`, so the surface echoes podman's own verb set, adding only what podman lacks for this niche (doctor as verb-zero, reap as the label-driven backstop) and expressing the ephemeral/persist split as flags on run rather than new nouns.
revisions.critique_of_others: MIRROR-PRIOR-ART (msb-tracking): Strongest verified claims of the three — I confirmed the exact clap enum in crates/cli/bin/main.rs, run.rs:224 "Remove unnamed (ephemeral) sandboxes", and spawn_reaper()/reap_stale_sandboxes(). But the lens overstates how close a "near-rename" graduation really is, and that is risky FOR THIS PROJECT. (1) msb's actual surface is NOT flat-and-minimal: it ships Run AND Create AND Start (a real two-phase lifecycle), plus Inspect, Metrics, Copy, Volume, Snapshot, Pull, Registry, Install, Ssh. The lens cherry-picks 7 verbs and calls that "tracking msb," but a future msb adopter will reach for `create`/`start`/`inspect`/`cp` and find they don't exist or are renamed — so the rename is not as near as advertised. (2) It folds persistence into `--keep`/`--name` flags on run, which directly contradicts the SAFETY lens's load-bearing point and weakens our locked "explicit opt-in" decision: a flag is the thing a clumsy agent fat-fingers. (3) It drops `inspect`, which podman/msb BOTH surface and which is the natural --json metadata contract verb; folding introspection into `status` loses the podman-native muscle memory the graduation story depends on. SAFETY-MINIMALIST: The split-verb persistence (`run` vs `persist`) is the single best idea in any proposal for THIS threat model — it makes the dangerous thing a different word, not a flag, which is exactly right for accident-not-adversary. But it has two real costs FOR THIS PROJECT. (1) `persist` is a coined verb that exists in NO prior art (msb/podman/smolvm/arrakis all use named-run or create/--detach); it maximizes safety but minimizes the zero-surprise familiarity my lens and the graduation signal both value, so a future msb/podman user must learn it. (2) Dropping `logs` and `inspect` is too aggressive: when a clumsy agent's webapp crashes in an ephemeral --rm sandbox, `logs` is the ONLY post-mortem (the container is gone), and `inspect` is how `--json` consumers read the worktree path / published port for the ssh/Caddy compose story the spec mandates. Folding everything into `ls` weakens the machine-readable-output locked decision. Also, `exec` with NO auto-start is defensible but diverges from msb's resolve_and_start, slightly hurting the graduation rename. Both other lenses correctly reject snapshot/volume/registry/install/network-DSL as out-of-threat-model and correctly insist on label-only selection — no disagreement there.
points_of_agreement: doctor is verb-zero, name taken verbatim from agent-sandbox (confirmed: agent-sandbox/crates/ags/src/cmd/doctor.rs); msb has no analog. It is read-only, fails CLOSED with a distinct precondition exit code and actionable message (usermod -aG kvm), and runs a --network none `true` smoke test under --runtime=krun.

reap is a FIRST-CLASS, user-invokable, label-driven backstop verb (not a hidden internal) AND also runs as a cheap reconcile sweep at the top of every invocation — modelled on microsandbox's spawn_reaper()/reap_stale_sandboxes() which I confirmed fires on every command (main.rs:307). It disarms the EXIT trap first to avoid double-cleanup, is idempotent, supports --dry-run and an --until/--older-than age GC off the sandbox.created label.

Selection is EXCLUSIVELY by the mandatory managed-by label set, never by name substring (the claude-code-sandbox anti-pattern). Every verb fails closed and touches only resources the tool stamped — never host-global state (the arrakis destroyAllVMs anti-pattern, confirmed in arrakis/cmd/restserver/main.go:122).
reap/rm worktree removal is path-safety-gated (inside managed root, not a symlink, not empty/root) and refuses to discard a worktree with unpushed/uncommitted commits unless forced.
The three-layer cleanup is locked: --rm clean exit (layer 1) + INT/TERM/EXIT trap calling `podman rm -f -v` (layer 2) + reap label backstop (layer 3).

I CONCEDE the core SAFETY point and revise toward it: persistence becomes a SEPARATE, louder verb rather than a --keep flag on run. `run` is unconditionally ephemeral (--rm always, no off switch). This overrides my original podman-native --keep flag because the accident threat model outweighs verb-count minimalism here.

snapshot/restore/pause/resume/volume/registry/install/metrics and any network-allowlist DSL are all dropped as out-of-threat-model (anti-exfiltration / state we refuse / breaks single-file auditability).
list/ps is label-scoped and machine-readable (--json / stable columns) BY DEFAULT, derived straight from `podman ps --format`.

ssh-agent forwarding (host SSH_AUTH_SOCK bound in, env var set) so keys never enter the guest; --network none and read-only-except-worktree are the safe defaults, stricter than podman's fail-open engine.
remaining_disagreements: VERB NAME for persistence. SAFETY uses the coined `persist`; I prefer `keep` as the verb (still a different word from `run`, so it satisfies SAFETY's split-verb requirement, but `keep` reads as the noun-less echo of podman/msb's named-persistence intent and is the term already floating in MIRROR's --keep). This is a soft disagreement; I will accept `persist` if the group does, since the load-bearing property (a DIFFERENT WORD, not a flag) is what matters and both satisfy it.

Whether `inspect` survives as a distinct verb. SAFETY folds it into `ls`; I hold that podman/docker/msb/krunvm/arrakis ALL surface `inspect` (confirmed in msb enum), it is the natural single-object --json metadata contract (worktree path, published localhost port) the ssh/Caddy compose story reads, and keeping it costs nothing (thin `podman inspect` passthrough). I keep it; MIRROR's `status` partially covers it but I prefer the podman-native name.

Whether `logs` survives. SAFETY drops it; I hold it is non-negotiable BECAUSE run is now always --rm: a crashed ephemeral workload leaves NO container to enter, so `podman logs` capture is the only post-mortem. MIRROR and I agree it stays.

Whether `exec` auto-starts a stopped persistent sandbox. MIRROR mirrors msb's resolve_and_start (auto-start); SAFETY refuses auto-start to keep lifetimes explicit. I side with SAFETY here for the accident model (no silent life-extension), accepting a small divergence from the msb graduation rename.

`cp` and `start`. I originally surfaced both as podman-native passthroughs. I now DROP `start` (with run always-ephemeral and persistence its own verb, a stopped-then-started lifecycle is marginal; if needed it belongs to the persist verb's domain). I keep `cp` as an optional thin convenience but concede it is the weakest verb and would drop it before crossing the single-file-auditability graduation line.

recommended_verbs.behavior: Verb zero, run before any launch and cheaply at the top of every other verb. Capability-probes the host: /dev/kvm exists and is accessible (KVM group membership), `crun --version` reports +LIBKRUN, the crun-krun handler/symlink is present, libkrun.so.1 and an ABI-compatible libkrunfw load, `podman info` succeeds, and a `podman --runtime crun-krun run --rm --network none <base> true` smoke test passes. Prints each gate PASS/FAIL with the validated podman/crun/libkrun versions.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Read-only: probes only, launches nothing persistent (smoke test is --rm --network none). Fails CLOSED with an actionable fix message (e.g. `usermod -aG kvm`) and a DISTINCT precondition exit code never conflated with a workload exit code. Exits 0 only if every gate passes.
recommended_verbs.provenance: Name and role verbatim from agent-sandbox `ags doctor` (crates/ags/src/cmd/doctor.rs); msb has no analog. Check content from crun +LIBKRUN feature string, libkrun capability probes (krun_check_nested_virt/krun_has_feature/krun_get_max_vcpus), libkrunfw ABI/soname check, and sbx's documented `usermod -aG kvm` first-run failure. UNANIMOUS across all three lenses, no daylight.

recommended_verbs.signature: sandbox run [--cpus N] [--memory MiB] [--network none|loopback] [--publish HOST:GUEST]... [--mount HOST:GUEST[:ro]]... [--env K=V]... [--workdir DIR] [--ssh-agent] [-it] [--timeout DUR] [--json] <image> [-- <cmd...>]
recommended_verbs.behavior: The primary one-shot verb and the only common path: create a fresh disposable microVM from <image> via `podman run --rm --runtime=krun`, create a per-sandbox git worktree on the host and bind it rw (:Z) at the workdir, run <cmd...> in the foreground (blocking), and propagate the workload exit code verbatim. UNCONDITIONALLY ephemeral: --rm is always on, there is no off switch on this verb. Persistence lives in the separate `keep` verb. Goes through the single centralized birth function that stamps the mandatory label set (sandbox.managed-by/.created/.id/.worktree) and the safe-by-default isolation flags.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: --rm always (locked ephemeral-by-default, no flag can flip it on this verb); --network none; all mounts read-only EXCEPT the single tool-created worktree (the only :rw, bound :Z); --security-opt no-new-privileges + conservative --cap-drop; conservative krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations (clamped >128 MiB, <=16 vCPU); --publish is a guarded no-op under --network none. Layer-1 (--rm clean exit) + Layer-2 (self-disarming trap on INT/TERM/EXIT -> `podman rm -f -v`). ssh-agent forwarded only on explicit --ssh-agent so keys never enter the guest.
recommended_verbs.provenance: `msb run <image> -- <cmd>` (crates/cli/lib/commands/run.rs; run.rs:224 removes unnamed/ephemeral sandboxes). Centralized birth + safe defaults converged unanimously. DIVERGES from msb's named-vs-unnamed-on-one-verb persistence: here run is purely ephemeral and persistence is a separate verb (see `keep`).

recommended_verbs.signature: sandbox keep --name N [--cpus N] [--memory MiB] [--network none|loopback] [--publish HOST:GUEST]... [--mount HOST:GUEST[:ro]]... [--env K=V]... [--workdir DIR] [--ssh-agent] [-it] [--timeout DUR] [--json] <image> [-- <cmd...>]
recommended_verbs.behavior: The explicit, louder persistence verb: create a microVM exactly like `run` but OMIT --rm so the sandbox survives exit, stamping sandbox.persist=true + sandbox.name in addition to the mandatory label set. A different WORD from run (not a flag on run), so the unsafe non-default path cannot be reached by fat-fingering a flag or by a stray --name flipping ephemeral->persistent. Requires --name.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Same centralized birth function and identical safe-by-default isolation as run (--network none, read-only-except-worktree, no-new-privileges, conservative clamped krun.cpus/ram_mib). Prints a LOUD warning that the sandbox survives and is the user's responsibility to `stop`/`rm`/`reap`. Still covered by the reap backstop if abandoned. SSH keys never enter the guest.
recommended_verbs.provenance: Persistence-as-separate-verb is SAFETY-MINIMALIST's load-bearing contribution; PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL CONCEDED to it and proposed the name `keep` over the coined `persist`. RECONCILED: 2-of-3 post-revision favor a separate verb; `keep` chosen over `persist` because it echoes msb/podman's named-persistence intent (and MIRROR's own --keep token) so the graduation rename to msb's named-run stays near, while still being a distinct word. The locked spec explicitly permits an opt-in VERB. MIRROR's --keep-flag position is the minority and is rejected (see rejected_verbs).

recommended_verbs.behavior: Re-start a previously kept, now-stopped sandbox via `podman start`, booting the microVM from its stored definition and reattaching. Exists ONLY to serve the persistent path created by `keep`; ephemeral `run` sandboxes never reach a startable stopped state.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Operates ONLY on sandboxes carrying our managed-by label; reuses the original isolation flags/annotations stamped at birth, opening no new mounts or network; refuses anything lacking our label.
recommended_verbs.provenance: podman/docker `start` verbatim; krunvm/smolvm/microsandbox (msb Start enum) all expose it. INCLUDED to close the concrete bug both SAFETY and PODMAN's no-start designs share: without start, a stopped kept sandbox can only be destroyed-and-recreated, ORPHANING its worktree -- the exact footgun worktree-per-sandbox exists to prevent. MIRROR's late inclusion is adopted; the create/start split is collapsed for the ephemeral path (run does it) but necessary for the persistent path.

recommended_verbs.signature: sandbox exec [--workdir DIR] [--env K=V]... [--user U] [-it] [--ssh-agent] [--timeout DUR] <name> -- <cmd...>
recommended_verbs.behavior: Run an additional command in an already-running kept sandbox; in tty mode with no `-- <cmd>` attaches a default shell. The 'enter the workload' path for the persistent case. Does NOT auto-start a stopped sandbox -- it fails closed and directs the user to `start`.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Operates ONLY on sandboxes carrying our managed-by label; inherits the parent's --network none and read-only posture, adds no new mounts; defaults to the workload user, not root. Refuses (fails closed) if the target is stopped rather than silently resurrecting it. ssh-agent forwards host SSH_AUTH_SOCK only on --ssh-agent so keys never enter the VM.
recommended_verbs.provenance: `msb exec <name> -- <cmd>` (crates/cli/lib/commands/exec.rs). DIVERGES deliberately from msb's resolve_and_start auto-start: 2-of-3 lenses (SAFETY + PODMAN) reject auto-start for the accident-not-adversary model -- silently extending a lifetime the user thought was over is the surprise to avoid. With an explicit `start` verb present the user is not stranded, so refusing auto-start costs nothing and keeps lifetimes explicit.

recommended_verbs.behavior: Stream or tail captured stdout/stderr from a running or exited sandbox via `podman logs`, for post-mortem on a crashed workload. Especially load-bearing because `run` is always --rm: a crashed ephemeral workload leaves no container to enter, so captured logs are the only post-mortem channel.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Read-only; label-scoped, refuses to dump logs of containers we don't manage. --tail defaults to a bounded line count.
recommended_verbs.provenance: `msb logs` (crates/cli/lib/commands/logs.rs); identical name/shape to podman, claude-code-sandbox `logs -f -n`. MIRROR and PODMAN hold it; SAFETY's drop is rejected -- PODMAN's argument that it is the ONLY post-mortem for an --rm'd crash is decisive.

recommended_verbs.behavior: List sandboxes managed by this tool by querying podman directly (`podman ps --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us>`), reconciling live state with no DB. Stable columns by default (id, status, persist-flag, published-localhost ports, worktree, created); --all includes exited/persistent. Also how a human or agent discovers what `reap` would act on.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Selection EXCLUSIVELY by the mandatory label set, never by name substring (the claude-code-sandbox anti-pattern). Read-only. Machine-readable (stable columns / --json from `podman ps --format`) is the DEFAULT, not an opt-in.
recommended_verbs.provenance: msb `List` with visible_alias `ls` (main.rs:59). PRIMARY name is `ls` (converging SAFETY's `ls` and PODMAN's `ps`-with-ls-alias instinct) -- shorter muscle-memory token shared across msb/sbx/smolvm/podman. UNANIMOUS in role.

recommended_verbs.behavior: Dump full configuration and runtime status of ONE sandbox (labels, annotations, OCIRuntime, mounts, published localhost ports, worktree path) via `podman inspect --format`. The single-object introspection counterpart to the many-row `ls`, and the --json metadata contract the ssh/Caddy compose story reads (worktree path, published localhost port).
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Read-only; label-gated to managed sandboxes; pure introspection, no side effects. --json emits the machine-readable single-object contract; exit code distinguishes not-found from found.
recommended_verbs.provenance: podman/docker `inspect` verbatim; msb Inspect enum, krunvm/arrakis all surface it. Chosen over MIRROR's original `status` name (podman users reach for `inspect` first; avoids `ps`-means-process-list confusion). SAFETY CONCEDED and added it; PODMAN held it; this is the post-deliberation consensus. Exactly ONE detail verb is kept (rejecting PODMAN's having BOTH ps AND inspect).

recommended_verbs.behavior: Gracefully stop running kept sandboxes -- SIGTERM then SIGKILL after timeout (`podman stop`). State and worktree are kept; does not remove. Moot for ephemeral `run` (they die in the foreground). --force kills immediately.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Label-scoped; graceful by default (grace before kill). Preserves the worktree (never deletes on stop). Warns rather than silently discarding a worktree with unpushed commits.
recommended_verbs.provenance: `msb stop -f -t` (crates/cli/lib/commands/stop.rs); same name in podman/smolvm/sbx/arrakis. msb/podman-native `stop` chosen over SAFETY's coined `down` for the near-rename and because stop (gentle) vs reap (nuclear) is already a clear two-rung ladder. SAFETY's `down` collapsed stop+rm; here they are kept distinct (a stopped kept sandbox is restartable via `start`).

recommended_verbs.behavior: Remove kept sandboxes from inventory (`podman rm -f -v`) and tear down their tool-created worktree. The explicit teardown for anything created with `keep`. --force stops-then-removes a running one.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Label-scoped. Worktree deletion guarded by a strict path-safety check (inside our managed root, not a symlink, not repo root, non-empty) and refuses/warns on unpushed commits unless forced; --keep-worktree preserves it.
recommended_verbs.provenance: msb `Remove` with visible_alias `rm` (main.rs:70-71); podman-native. Worktree-loss guard answers sbx's documented `rm` footgun and smolvm's is_safe_cache_path. UNANIMOUS in role across all three lenses.

recommended_verbs.behavior: Label-driven cleanup BACKSTOP (Layer 3) for the crash cases --rm and traps miss (SIGKILLed run, VMM segfault, an abandoned kept sandbox): `podman ps -a -q --filter label=sandbox.managed-by=<us> --filter status=exited` then `podman rm -f -v`, with an optional age cut via the sandbox.created label / --until. Runs as a cheap reconcile at the top of every invocation (so the field stays clean without a daemon) AND is directly invokable so the backstop is auditable, not hidden.
recommended_verbs.safe_default: Touches ONLY containers carrying our managed-by label -- never host-global state (the arrakis destroyAllVMs/tap*/br0 anti-pattern). Idempotent. Disarms the EXIT trap before sweeping (gh-runner lesson). --dry-run previews. Skips healthy running sandboxes and kept ones unless dead/orphaned. Same path-guarded + unpushed-commit-guarded worktree removal as `rm`.
recommended_verbs.provenance: Severity-ladder name from claude-code-sandbox `purge`/`clean` (collapsed to ONE reap), modelled on microsandbox reap_stale_sandboxes()/spawn_reaper() (fires every command, main.rs:307) and gh-runner-krunvm prefix-reaper, re-keyed to LABELS. THE SINGLE STRONGEST CONSENSUS -- first-class verb, unanimous across all three lenses.

rejected_verbs.why: PODMAN-MENTAL-MODEL's weakest verb; SAFETY and MIRROR reject it and PODMAN conceded it would be the first dropped at the graduation line. `podman cp` into a running krun microVM is a mount-surface-adjacent ingress/egress channel that fights 'read-only mounts where possible'. The tool-created worktree (git push out) is the sanctioned artifact channel for an accident-containment model; cp is pure extra mutation surface and verb count for no threat-model gain.

The persistence verb IS adopted, but under the name `keep`, not the coined `persist`. `persist` exists in no prior art (msb/podman/smolvm/arrakis), maximizing safety but minimizing the zero-surprise familiarity the graduation rename depends on. `keep` is still a distinct word (satisfying SAFETY's load-bearing split-verb requirement) while echoing msb/podman named-persistence intent. PODMAN proposed this and SAFETY accepts any distinct word; the property that matters is a DIFFERENT WORD, not a flag.
