MIRROR-PRIOR-ART's flag-based persistence is rejected (minority post-revision: PODMAN conceded away from it). msb's own mechanism is the hazard -- a stray --name flips a run from ephemeral to persistent. For accident-not-adversary, persistence must be an unmistakable different word, not a flag a clumsy agent can fat-finger or a default that flips on stray args. The locked spec permits an opt-in verb; the separate verb makes the unsafe path unexpressible-by-accident.

rejected_verbs.verb: status (as a second introspection verb) / ps duplication
rejected_verbs.why: Introspection is collapsed to exactly ONE detail verb (`inspect`) plus the many-row `ls`. MIRROR's original `status` is renamed to the podman-native `inspect`; PODMAN's instinct to surface BOTH ps AND inspect is rejected -- two introspection verbs is more surface for no threat-model gain.

msb ships Create AND Run AND Start. The ephemeral path collapses create+start into `run`, and the persistent path uses `keep`(create+run) + `start`. A standalone `create` (build-but-don't-run) adds a stopped-never-started state that only multiplies reap's orphan-tracking burden with no accident-model benefit.

rejected_verbs.verb: snapshot / restore / pause / resume / volume / registry / pull / install / login / config / ssh / metrics / network-policy-DSL
rejected_verbs.why: All out of scope, unanimous across all three lenses. Out of the accident-not-adversary threat model (snapshot/pause/metrics), violate no-state-file / no-login / no-daemon (registry/install/login/config), or are anti-exfiltration surface (network-allowlist DSL) the project explicitly does not pursue. ssh-into-guest is unneeded since ssh-agent forwarding keeps keys out and exec/run are the entry paths. Dropping these IS the single-file-auditability graduation discipline. Tailscale/remote/host concerns stay orthogonal -- NO --host flag.

global_flags: --json: machine-readable output on every read verb (doctor, ls, inspect, reap --dry-run); a stable single-object/array contract derived from `podman ... --format`. Locked machine-readable-output decision; on by spirit for listing verbs (stable columns are the default even without --json).
--help/-h: per-verb usage; flag-preceded, scriptable arg parsing.
-it: interactive TTY allocation, shared by run/keep/start/exec.
--ssh-agent: opt-in host SSH_AUTH_SOCK forwarding (run/keep/exec) so keys never enter the guest; never on by default.
--network none|loopback: safe default none on every birth verb; loopback only when explicitly requested; --publish is a guarded no-op under none.
--cpus N / --memory MiB: map to conservative, clamped krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib annotations on birth verbs.
--force/-f: explicit affirmative override for guarded teardown (stop/rm), required to discard a worktree with unpushed commits.
--dry-run: preview without mutation (reap; extendable to rm).

Distinct, stable exit codes: a DISTINCT precondition-failure code (doctor and the per-verb precheck) that is NEVER conflated with a propagated workload exit code; not-found vs found distinguished on inspect; stable across releases.
open_questions: Worktree creation contract: exact managed root path (e.g. ~/.local/share/sandbox/worktrees/<id>), naming scheme keyed to sandbox.id vs sandbox.name, and the branch/checkout policy when `keep`/`run` create the worktree (new branch per sandbox? detached? from which ref?). The destructive-git guard's precise refusal rules (repo root, symlink, non-empty, unpushed/uncommitted) need concrete predicates.

reap-on-every-invocation cost and concurrency: running the reconcile sweep at the top of every verb must be cheap and must not race two concurrent invocations into double-rm or trap/reap double-cleanup. Confirm the EXIT-trap-disarm-before-sweep ordering holds under concurrent runs, since there is no state file/lock by design. This is also a named graduation signal (concurrency-safe allocation) -- decide the threshold at which bash stops sufficing.

--network loopback semantics under krun: confirm what 'loopback' maps to (pasta/slirp4netns? host-loopback-only?) and how --publish HOST:GUEST binds predictably to localhost for the ssh/Caddy compose story without a --host flag, including behavior when none is selected.

libkrunfw ABI/soname compatibility matrix: which exact libkrun.so.1 / libkrunfw soname versions doctor should accept vs reject on Fedora, and whether the krun_* capability probes are callable from bash (likely need a tiny helper) or must be inferred from `crun features` + version strings.

exec/start label-gating UX: precise error text and exit code when exec targets a stopped kept sandbox (fail-closed, no auto-start) so the agent reliably learns to call `start` -- this is the deliberate divergence from msb and must be discoverable.
krun.cpus/krun.ram_mib clamp bounds: confirm the concrete floor/ceiling (>128 MiB, <=16 vCPU were proposed) against libkrun's krun_get_max_vcpus and Fedora host realities.

Base image policy: doctor's smoke test and run's default <image> -- is there a canonical Fedora base the tool assumes/pulls, and does pulling violate the no-registry-verb stance (pull-on-run is implicit, not a verb)?
synthesis.summary: Recommended surface: ELEVEN verbs -- doctor, run, keep, start, exec, logs, ls, inspect, stop, rm, reap -- a thin disciplined wrapper over `podman run --runtime=krun` where podman labels are the only source of truth (no daemon, no state file). The spine is the unanimous consensus: doctor is verb-zero (read-only, fail-closed, distinct precondition exit code, actionable `usermod -aG kvm`) and runs cheaply before every other verb; reap is a first-class label-driven Layer-3 backstop that also reconciles at the top of every invocation; every verb selects EXCLUSIVELY by the mandatory sandbox.managed-by label set (never name substring); safe-by-default isolation is uniform (--network none, read-only-except-the-one-tool-created-worktree, no-new-privileges, conservative clamped krun.cpus/ram_mib, ssh-agent forwarding so keys never enter the guest); listing is machine-readable by default; the tool CREATES the worktree and all worktree removal is path-safety-gated AND unpushed-commit-guarded. The two genuine disagreements are resolved by post-deliberation weight rather than by the original three-way split. (1) PERSISTENCE: a separate verb `keep`, not a `--keep` flag on run. SAFETY-MINIMALIST's split-verb argument is the strongest single idea for an accident-not-adversary model (a different WORD cannot be fat-fingered the way msb's stray --name flips ephemeral->persistent), PODMAN explicitly conceded to it, and the locked spec permits an opt-in verb -- so `run` is now UNCONDITIONALLY ephemeral (--rm, no off switch) and `keep --name` is the louder, labelled, warned persistence path. The name `keep` (PODMAN's proposal) is chosen over the coined `persist` to preserve the msb-graduation near-rename. (2) `start` IS included (MIRROR's late correction): without it a stopped kept sandbox can only be destroyed-and-recreated, orphaning its worktree -- the precise footgun worktree-per-sandbox exists to prevent; this is the concrete bug in both no-start designs. exec deliberately does NOT auto-start (2-of-3, diverging from msb's resolve_and_start) -- with `start` present, refusing silent resurrection costs nothing and keeps lifetimes explicit. logs and inspect both survive (rejecting SAFETY's drops): logs is the only post-mortem for an always---rm crash, and inspect is the single-object --json contract the ssh/Caddy compose story reads. REJECTED: `cp` (mount-surface-adjacent egress that the worktree/git-push channel already covers), a flag-based persistence, a second introspection verb, standalone `create`, and the whole snapshot/volume/registry/install/login/config/network-DSL family -- dropping them IS the single-file-auditability graduation discipline. Build-stage must resolve the worktree creation/branch contract, the no-lock concurrency behavior of reap-on-every-invocation, --network loopback/--publish-to-localhost semantics under krun, and the exact libkrunfw ABI acceptance matrix for doctor."
